content: Just as 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems prioritize selectively retaining high-information, curiosity-primed experiences to avoid storage bloat and improve generalization, general AI agents can reorient their core action selection loop to prune low-value interaction paths the same way active memory prunes low-information memories. This creates a 'memory-first agent architecture' where memory curation drives long-term task adaptation, not just episodic recall.
